title: Llama7B clustered attention schedule with full-value measured L1 tile costs
hypothesis: Replacing the folded local L1 attention proxy with the measured full-value value-datapath tile costs will change the feasible cluster-count and die-size frontier, especially where local cluster logic area competes with SRAM capacity.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the L2 Llama7B 131k clustered attention frontier selected with folded L1 proxy costs hold after charging the measured full-value L1 tile datapath PPA?
include: native GQA KV8 Llama7B at 131072 context length
measured full-value L1 tile profiles from the L1 closure run
NoC bandwidth and hop sensitivity matching the folded measured-L1 schedule run

expected_benefit: revises the current Llama7B attention frontier using less abstract local tile PPA
quantifies whether the folded-proxy winner was optimistic on local logic area
selects the next integrated L1/L2 attention measurement point
risks: full-value tile profiles still abstract softmax-weight generation
the clustered schedule model charges local area and clock but not routed SRAM macros
NoC contention remains bandwidth/hop based rather than cycle accurate

required_evaluations.objective: Rerun the Llama7B 131k clustered attention schedule using full-value measured L1 tile costs instead of folded proxy costs.

expected_result.reason: Use the full-value measured-L1 schedule frontier to choose the next concrete attention memory/NoC integration point.
